messages.content:
Most new teachers are most worried about:
Group of answer choices

meeting the expectations of the parents of students

developing expertise through experience and knowledge

providing clear feedback to students

class management and discipline
All of the given options can be concerns for new teachers, but class management and discipline are often the most common worries.

In a physical activity class, engaged time is the:
Group of answer choices

time actually spent on task, doing physical activity

time from the beginning of class until the students change clothes

time spent stretching

time from the beginning of class to the end

Engaged time in a physical activity class refers to the time actually spent on task, doing physical activity. It is the time where the students are actively participating in the physical activities, not including any transition time or time spent on other non-active tasks.

What is the primary goal of a physical activity pedagogist? 
Group of answer choices

to research

to educate

to win

to retain

The primary goal of a physical activity pedagogist is to educate. Their role is to teach and facilitate learning in the field of physical activity and promote the development of knowledge, skills, and attitudes related to physical education and physical fitness.

The art, science and profession of teaching is known as:
Group of answer choices

curricular models

pedagogy

academic performance

overload
The art, science, and profession of teaching is known as pedagogy.

The relationship between physical activity and academic performance has all of the following components EXCEPT:
Group of answer choices

Academic achievement

Grade inflation

Academic behaviors

Cognitive skills and attitudes

The relationship between physical activity and academic performance has all of the listed components except for "Grade inflation." Grade inflation refers to the practice of giving higher grades than deserved to students, and it is not a component of the relationship between physical activity and academic performance.

Public Law 94-142 ensures that:
Group of answer choices

students from every neighborhood demographic receive physical education

teachers use the same curriculum for every class

students with disabilities are provided appropriate education

all students receive equal attention from teachers

Public Law 94-142, also known as the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA), ensures that students with disabilities are provided appropriate education. It mandates that students with disabilities have access to a free and appropriate public education in the least restrictive environment possible. The law guarantees that these students receive special education services and supports tailored to their individual needs. It does not specifically address physical education or ensure that students from every neighborhood demographic receive physical education. It is focused on providing equal educational opportunities for students with disabilities.

Effective teachers are those who do ALL of the following EXCEPT:
Group of answer choices

relies mostly on personal experiences

implements interesting curriculum

provides appropriate practice

uses principles that produce the best student learning

Effective teachers are those who implement interesting curriculum, provide appropriate practice, and use principles that produce the best student learning. However, they do not rely mostly on personal experiences. Instead, effective teachers draw upon a range of resources, including research-based instructional strategies, professional development, and collaboration with colleagues, to inform their teaching practices. They take into account both their own experiences and the evidence-based practices in order to best meet the needs of their students.

To work as an athletic trainer, _________________ states require licensure.
Group of answer choices

Most, but not all.

None

Some

All

To work as an athletic trainer, most, but not all states require licensure. Licensure requirements and regulations can vary from state to state, and while the majority of states do mandate licensure for athletic trainers, there are a few states that may not have licensure requirements in place.

Who would you contact to get fitted for a wrist brace? 
Group of answer choices

orthotist

occupational therapist

physical therapist

therapeutic recreation specialist

For fitting a wrist brace, you would generally contact an orthotist. Orthotists are healthcare professionals who specialize in the design and fitting of orthoses, which are devices like braces or supports used to provide external support, stability, or protection to a body part. They are trained to assess and recommend the appropriate orthotic device for your specific needs, including wrist braces.

Which of the following is NOT an example of a client population for rehabilitative therapeutic exercise?
Group of answer choices

patients with postsurgical trauma

cardiopulmonary patients

individuals with psychological disorders

children with developmental disorders

Cardiopulmonary patients are not typically considered a client population for rehabilitative therapeutic exercise. Cardiopulmonary rehabilitation focuses on improving cardiovascular and respiratory function and is not directly related to rehabilitative therapeutic exercise, which primarily addresses musculoskeletal and functional impairments.

What emerging trend in health care will need to be watched by therapeutic exercise professionals in the future? 
Group of answer choices

carpal tunnel syndrome

the declining function of the majority of the population

angry patients and clients who don't like to take orders

remote home health care

The emerging trend in health care that will need to be watched by therapeutic exercise professionals in the future is remote home health care. With advancements in technology and telemedicine, more and more health care services are being delivered remotely, including therapeutic exercise. This trend allows for increased access to care, convenience, and monitoring of patients in their own homes. It is important for therapeutic exercise professionals to stay informed and adapt their practices to effectively provide remote services and support to patients.

Historically, when someone had and acute orthopedic injury, the body part would be immobilized, the trend now is:
Group of answer choices

Immobilize the body part for as long as it takes to heal.

Immobilize, such as a cast for 2 weeks, then put through a reduced range of motion

Immediately after an injury, place gentle loads on the tissue.

A short time after an injury, put the joint through a full range of motion

The trend now for acute orthopedic injuries is to immediately place gentle loads on the tissue. Instead of complete immobilization, there is growing recognition of the benefits of early mobilization and movement to promote healing and prevent complications such as muscle atrophy and joint stiffness. This approach often involves early rehabilitation protocols that include controlled movements and exercises to encourage tissue healing and restore function.

Most new teachers are most worried about:
Group of answer choices

meeting the expectations of the parents of students

developing expertise through experience and knowledge

providing clear feedback to students

class management and discipline

While all of the given options can be concerns for new teachers, class management and discipline are often the most common worries. New teachers may be concerned about maintaining order in the classroom, managing student behavior, and establishing effective routines and procedures. This is because creating a positive and productive learning environment is crucial for student engagement and academic success.

The internship is a valuable part of your education because it allows you to apply your skills in a supervised setting, and it serves as a test of your preparedness for your profession. 
Group of answer choices

true

false

True. The statement accurately describes the value of an internship in education. It provides students with the opportunity to apply their skills and knowledge in a real-world setting under supervision, allowing them to gain practical experience and assess their readiness for their chosen profession.

According to your text, realistic personality types are a good fit for the field of kinesiology.
Group of answer choices

Only as teachers.

False

True

False. I apologize for the confusion, but as an AI language model, I don't have access to specific texts. However, the suitability of a realistic personality type for the field of kinesiology may vary based on individual preferences and strengths. While having a practical and hands-on approach can be beneficial in certain areas of kinesiology, such as exercise science or athletic training, it is not a definitive requirement or exclusive to the field. Other personality traits and skills, such as communication, empathy, and critical thinking, are also valuable in the field of kinesiology.

According to the text, volunteering:
Group of answer choices

socialize you into the field

provide practical experience

can demonstrate a willingness to serve others

All of these are outcomes of volunteering.

All of these are outcomes of volunteering. Volunteering can socialize individuals into a particular field, provide them with practical experience, and demonstrate a willingness to serve others. It can offer valuable opportunities for individuals to gain hands-on experience, develop professional connections, and contribute to their communities or causes they are passionate about.

Professionals are granted monopolies on the delivery of services because only they possess the knowledge and skills to meet the particular needs of the community.
Group of answer choices

until Title Nine was passed

only in politics

true

false

False. While professionals often possess specialized knowledge and skills, the statement incorrectly suggests that professionals are granted monopolies on the delivery of services solely because of their unique abilities. The regulation of professional practice and the granting of licenses or certifications is usually done to ensure that professionals meet certain standards of competency and ethics to protect the public. It is a mechanism to ensure the quality and safety of the services provided by professionals rather than solely based on their knowledge and skills. Additionally, the statement's mention of Title Nine is unrelated to the question and does not provide any context or relevance.

Fitness leaders and physical education teachers are expected to model a healthy lifestyle and maintain an appropriate fitness level, this is an example of:
Group of answer choices

physical activity professional standards

grounded theory

health and medicine career integration

market-driven professionalism

This is an example of physical activity professional standards. Physical activity professionals, such as fitness leaders and physical education teachers, are expected to model a healthy lifestyle and maintain an appropriate fitness level as part of their professional responsibilities. This expectation is outlined in professional standards and guidelines that establish the expectations and behaviors for professionals in the field of physical activity.

According to your text, the type of knowledge represented by a fitness leader's skill in motivating his clients to adhere to a disciplined exercise routine, and one that is very difficult for the leader to explain to others, is called:
Group of answer choices

applied theoretical knowledge

workplace knowledge

theoretical kinesiology knowledge

professional practice knowledge

According to the information available to me, the type of knowledge represented by a fitness leader's skill in motivating clients to adhere to a disciplined exercise routine, and one that is difficult to explain, is referred to as professional practice knowledge. Professional practice knowledge encompasses the practical expertise, insights, and strategies that professionals have developed through their experiences and interactions in their specific field. It is knowledge that is often gained through years of hands-on practice and may be challenging to articulate or transfer to others.

A skillful orthopedist who performs many unnecessary operations on patients in order to make more money is an example of what type of professional?
Group of answer choices

not a professional

medical professional

mechanical, market-driven professional

social trustee, civic professional

A skillful orthopedist who performs many unnecessary operations on patients in order to make more money would be an example of a mechanical, market-driven professional. This type of professional prioritizes financial gain and profit over the well-being and best interests of their patients. They may exploit their professional skills and knowledge for personal financial gain, disregarding ethical considerations and the welfare of their patients.

The components of the multidimensional model of wellness are:
Group of answer choices

physical, emotional, environmental, cognitive, and medical

physical, intellectual, emotional, social, and spiritual

behavioral, existential, social, physical, and environmental

behavioral, physical, emotional, educational, and spiritual

The components of the multidimensional model of wellness are typically described as physical, intellectual, emotional, social, and spiritual. These dimensions encompass various aspects of overall well-being and highlight the interconnectedness of different areas of life that contribute to a person's holistic wellness.

Worksite fitness and health programs have been found to:
Group of answer choices

increase absenteeism

reduce medical claims

reduce employee satisfaction

All of these are outcomes of employee wellness programming..

Worksite fitness and health programs have been found to reduce medical claims and increase employee satisfaction. These programs are designed to promote the well-being of employees through various initiatives such as fitness activities, education on healthy behaviors, stress management, and more. By implementing these programs, organizations can help employees improve their health and reduce the likelihood of medical issues, leading to decreased medical claims. Additionally, providing wellness programs can improve overall employee satisfaction by creating a positive and supportive work environment that prioritizes employee well-being.

However, worksite fitness and health programs are not expected to increase absenteeism. On the contrary, these programs aim to enhance employee health and engagement, potentially reducing absenteeism due to illness or stress-related issues.

In 2008, the U.S. government built on the influential 1996 Surgeon General's report with a document entitled:
Group of answer choices

Healthy People 2008

Physical activity guidelines for Americans

Leisure and Health in America

2008 Surgeon General's Report

In 2008, the U.S. government built on the influential 1996 Surgeon General's report with a document entitled "Physical Activity Guidelines for Americans." This updated report provided evidence-based recommendations and guidelines for physical activity and its health benefits, aiming to promote and encourage regular physical activity among Americans to improve overall health and well-being. The document emphasized the importance of regular physical activity across various age groups and populations.

Your text makes the claim that physical activity is:
Group of answer choices

Integral to societal well-being

All of these are true of physical activity.

Mainly a priority for those who are overweight or obese.

Important mainly for children

As an AI language model, I don't have access to specific texts. However, I can provide general information. Physical activity is generally considered integral to societal well-being. It offers numerous health benefits for individuals of all ages, such as improved cardiovascular health, stronger bones and muscles, enhanced mental well-being, and reduced risk of various chronic diseases. Engaging in regular physical activity is important for maintaining overall health and quality of life. While physical activity is beneficial for those who are overweight or obese, it is relevant for individuals of all body types and weight statuses. It is not solely a priority for those who are overweight or obese but should be encouraged for everyone, including children, adults, and older adults, to lead healthy and active lives.

Your text makes the claim that physical activity should be viewed as:
Group of answer choices

Important mainly for senior citizens

a powerful strategy for disease prevention

optional and recreational endeavor

appopriate mainly for those who have sufficient time away from work

I apologize for any confusion, but as an AI language model, my responses are generated based on a mixture of licensed data, data created by human trainers, and publicly available data. I have not been directly trained on specific texts that make claims about physical activity. However, I can provide some general information.

Physical activity should be viewed as a powerful strategy for disease prevention. Engaging in regular physical activity has been associated with a reduced risk of various chronic diseases, including cardiovascular disease, diabetes, certain cancers, and mental health disorders. It is recommended for individuals of all ages to incorporate physical activity into their daily routines to improve overall health and well-being.

It is important to note that physical activity is not limited to any specific age group or population. It is relevant for people of all ages, from children to senior citizens. While the benefits and types of physical activity may vary based on individual needs and capabilities, regular physical activity is encouraged for everyone.

Physical activity should not be considered optional or solely a recreational endeavor. It is an essential component of a healthy lifestyle and an important aspect of overall well-being.

Students aspiring to health and fitness careers should specialize in biophysical subdisciplines and not worry about taking courses in teaching, counseling, business, and marketing. 
Group of answer choices

True

False

False. Students aspiring to health and fitness careers should not only focus on biophysical subdisciplines but should also consider taking courses in teaching, counseling, business, and marketing. These additional courses can provide valuable skills and knowledge that are essential for success in health and fitness careers. Teaching courses can help develop communication and instructional strategies, counseling courses can enhance understanding of behavior change and client support, and business and marketing courses can provide entrepreneurial and management skills necessary for running health and fitness-related enterprises. A well-rounded education that includes a range of disciplines can better equip individuals for the complexities and demands of health and fitness careers.

Which is NOT a professional career in fitness?
Group of answer choices

university baseball coach

health and fitness director

group exercise instructor

personal trainer

A university baseball coach is not typically considered a professional career in fitness. While coaching sports activities may involve elements of fitness and physical training, the term "professional career in fitness" usually refers to roles directly involved in training, guiding, and promoting individual or group fitness activities. Examples of professional careers in fitness include health and fitness directors, group exercise instructors, and personal trainers.

Improving health by reducing the illness and disease burden of an entire county, city or state by working with health departments is known as:
Group of answer choices

Disease reduction approach

Population-based health

Public monitoring model

Health and wellness coaching

Improving health by reducing the illness and disease burden of an entire county, city, or state by working with health departments is known as population-based health. This approach focuses on the health of the entire population and aims to prevent and manage diseases, promote health behaviors, and improve overall population health outcomes. It involves collaboration among health professionals, community organizations, and government agencies to implement interventions and policies that address the health needs of the population at a broader level.

According to your text, researchers in kinesiology careers have a/n:
Group of answer choices

civic outlook

artistic personality

All of these.

investigative personality

According to the information available to me, researchers in kinesiology careers often have an investigative personality. The field of kinesiology involves scientific inquiry and research to understand human movement, exercise, and physical activity. Researchers in this field typically possess a passion for investigation, critical thinking, and the scientific method to advance knowledge and contribute to the field. While individuals in kinesiology careers may also have a range of other personality traits, such as a civic outlook or an interest in the arts, having an investigative personality is particularly relevant for research in kinesiology.

According to your text, as a rule, professional advancement:
Group of answer choices

is tightly linked to number of hours in a work week.

comes from avoiding administrative responsibilities

All of these are true of professional advancement.

is closely connected to educational attainment

According to the information available to me, professional advancement in most fields, including but not limited to kinesiology, is closely connected to educational attainment. Acquiring advanced degrees, certifications, and specialized training can significantly contribute to career progression and open up opportunities for higher-level positions, increased
